curriculum.subject: Science (086)
curriculum.overallDescription: 16 units, 16 chapters, 2 topics, 0 subtopics, and 34 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table.
Explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).
Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances including distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation.
Identifies types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions.
Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations.
Analyzes acid-base reactions, metal-non-metal interactions, and reversible/irreversible changes.
Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces on state of motion (displacement, direction, velocity, acceleration).
Analyzes graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension including uniform circular motion and acceleration due to gravity.
Explains the relationship between mass and weight using the universal law of gravitation.
Connects gravitational laws to the laws of motion.
Manipulates the position of objects and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics.
Corresponds observations with ray diagrams and extends understanding to combinations of lenses (telescope, microscope).

question_paper_design: Competency: Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (20%)
Typology of Questions: VSA (including objective type), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions
bloom_levels: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize (Knowledge)
Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish (Application)
Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct (Analysis/Evaluation/Creation)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments: 14 specific experiments covering pH determination, acid-base properties, reaction classification, reactivity series, Ohm's law verification, resistance determination, stomata observation, CO2 respiration, acetic acid properties, soap cleaning capacity, focal length determination, light refraction through prism, binary fission/budding observation, and embryo identification
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science (CBSE Publication)
project_assessment: Portfolio: Brief write-up on topics not assessed in year-end examination (Periodic Classification of Elements; Heredity and Evolution; Electric Effects of Electric Current)
Credit given for Periodic Assessment/Portfolio based on joyful and experiential opportunities

C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)
C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

competency_groups.title: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level
competency_groups.description: Describes classification of elements, explains compound formation based on atomic structure, investigates nature of chemical substances, and represents chemical interactions using equations.
competencies: C 1.1 – Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).

C 1.2 – Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions)
C 1.3 – Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible)
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour

competency_groups.title: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
competency_groups.description: Applies Newton's laws to explain forces and motion, explains mass-weight relationships, manipulates lenses to observe image characteristics, analyses circuit characteristics, defines work and energy relationships, demonstrates mechanical advantage, describes sound properties, and explores interconnected Earth systems.
competencies: C 2.1 – Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
C 2.2 – Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation, and connect it to the laws of motion

C 2.3 – Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)

C 2.4 – Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
C 2.5 – Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions
C 2.6 – Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys)
C 2.7 – Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments

C 2.8 – Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere and their interrelationships, earth processes, hazards, etc.)

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competency_groups.description: Explains the role of cellular components, analyses life processes (nutrition, transport, exchange, reproduction), and describes mechanisms of heredity.
competencies: C 3.1 – Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competency_groups.description: Applies knowledge of cellular diversity to classify organisms, illustrates levels of organisation, analyses biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems, analyses patterns of inheritance, and analyses evidence of biological evolution.
competencies: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competency_groups.description: Explores influence of literature and arts on Science, examines case studies from Social Sciences and Ethics perspective, and applies scientific principles to phenomena in other subjects.
competencies: C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science

C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (for example, Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity)
C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history, and the present time to the overall field of Science
competency_groups.description: Knows and explains significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner.
competencies: C 6.1 – Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge
competency_groups.description: States concepts representing current understanding and states questions related to matters where current scientific understanding is well-recognised.
competencies: C 7.1 – States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied, ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C 7.2 – States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competency_groups.description: Develops accurate models to represent real-life events and predict results, and designs/implements plans for scientific inquiry including hypothesis formulation, data representation, and communication.
competencies: C 8.1 – Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles, and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C 8.2 – Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data, and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws and principles, and communicates findings using scientific terminology)

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
learning_outcomes.topic: Classification of Elements and Compound Formation
outcomes: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table.
Explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
learning_outcomes.topic: Nature and Properties of Chemical Substances
outcomes: Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances including distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation.
Identifies types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions.

learning_outcomes.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
outcomes: Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations.
Analyzes acid-base reactions, metal-non-metal interactions, and reversible/irreversible changes.
Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces on state of motion (displacement, direction, velocity, acceleration).
Analyzes graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension including uniform circular motion and acceleration due to gravity.
Explains the relationship between mass and weight using the universal law of gravitation.
Connects gravitational laws to the laws of motion.
Manipulates the position of objects and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics.
Corresponds observations with ray diagrams and extends understanding to combinations of lenses (telescope, microscope).
Manipulates and analyses circuit characteristics (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship using Ohm's law.
Applies electrical concepts to everyday usage including electricity bills, short circuits, and safety measures.
Defines work in scientific terms and represents relationships between potential and kinetic energy mathematically.
Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (levers and pulleys).
Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude).
Analyzes differences in sound perception as it propagates through different instruments.
Explores interconnected systems and phenomena supporting life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere).
Analyzes interrelationships between earth processes and hazards.
Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall).
Describes the semi-permeability of the cell membrane as the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes.
Analyses similarities and differences in life processes including nutrition (photosynthesis, absorption, digestion), transport, exchange of materials, and reproduction.
Compares mechanisms across plants, fungi, and animals.
Describes mechanisms of heredity in terms of DNA, genes, and chromosomes.
Explains variation as changes in the sequence of DNA.
Applies knowledge of cellular diversity and ecological roles to classify organisms into five kingdoms.
Illustrates different levels of organisation of living organisms from molecules to organisms.
Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes.
Examines interactions taking place at each level of organisation.
Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws.
Evaluates consequences at a population level using models and/or simulations.
Analyses evidence of biological evolution demonstrating consequences of natural selection.
Describes changes in allele frequency, structure, and function of organisms.

activities.title: Studying the properties of acids and bases on reaction with indicators and metals
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.chapter_name: Chemical Reactions and Equations / Acids, Bases and Salts
activities.details: Observe reactions of HCl and NaOH with litmus solution (blue/red), zinc metal, and solid sodium carbonate to identify properties.

activities.title: Classifying chemical reactions through observation
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Perform and observe combination, decomposition, displacement, and double displacement reactions including action of water on quicklime and heat on ferrous sulphate crystals.

activities.title: Determining reactivity series of metals
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3) to arrange metals in decreasing order of reactivity.

activities.title: Comparative cleaning capacity of soap
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Study the cleaning capacity of a soap sample in soft water versus hard water.

activities.title: Tracing light path through a glass slab
activities.unit_name: Unit III: Natural Phenomena
activities.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence and measure angles of incidence, refraction, and emergence.

activities.title: Tracing light path through a prism
activities.unit_name: Unit III: Natural Phenomena
activities.details: Trace the path of rays of light passing through a glass prism to observe refraction and dispersion.

activities.title: Portfolio write-up on non-assessed topics
activities.unit_name: Unit I, Unit II, Unit IV
activities.chapter_name: Periodic Classification of Elements / Heredity and Evolution / Electric Effects
activities.details: Prepare a brief write-up in the portfolio for topics included formatively (Periodic Classification, Heredity/Evolution, Electric Effects) to reinforce understanding without summative assessment.

projects.title: Scientific Inquiry Plan Design
projects.unit_name: Unit VIII: Nature of Science by Doing Science
projects.chapter_name: Nature of Science by Doing Science
projects.details: Design and implement a plan for scientific inquiry involving hypothesis formulation, variable identification, accurate instrument use, data representation in multiple modes, and communication of findings using scientific terminology.

projects.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
projects.unit_name: Unit V: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
projects.chapter_name: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
projects.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, green revolution, GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

practicals.title: Finding pH of various samples
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Studying dependence of potential difference on current
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Determination of equivalent resistance
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Preparing temporary mount of leaf peel
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Showing CO2 release during respiration
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Studying properties of acetic acid
practicals.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
practicals.details: Study the odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate of acetic acid (ethanoic acid).

practicals.title: Determination of focal length
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Studying binary fission and budding
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Identification of embryo parts
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Classifying chemical reactions through observation
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Perform and observe combination, decomposition, displacement, and double displacement reactions including action of water on quicklime and heat on ferrous sulphate crystals.

activities.title: Comparative cleaning capacity of soap
activities.unit_name: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour
activities.details: Study the cleaning capacity of a soap sample in soft water versus hard water.

activities.unit_name: Unit III: Natural Phenomena
activities.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence and measure angles of incidence, refraction, and emergence.

activities.title: Tracing light path through a prism
activities.unit_name: Unit III: Natural Phenomena
activities.details: Trace the path of rays of light passing through a glass prism to observe refraction and dispersion.

projects.title: Scientific Inquiry Plan Design
projects.unit_name: Unit VIII: Nature of Science by Doing Science
projects.chapter_name: Nature of Science by Doing Science

projects.chapter_name: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas
projects.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, green revolution, GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour (25 Marks)
Unit II: World of Living (25 Marks)
Unit IV: Effects of Current (13 Marks)
question_paper_design: Competency: Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (20%)
Typology of Questions: VSA (including objective type), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions
bloom_levels: State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize (Knowledge)
Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish (Application)
Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct (Analysis/Evaluation/Creation)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments: 14 specific experiments covering pH determination, acid-base properties, reaction classification, reactivity series, Ohm's law verification, resistance determination, stomata observation, CO2 respiration, acetic acid properties, soap cleaning capacity, focal length determination, light refraction through prism, binary fission/budding observation, and embryo identification
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science (CBSE Publication)
project_assessment: Portfolio: Brief write-up on topics not assessed in year-end examination (Periodic Classification of Elements; Heredity and Evolution; Electric Effects of Electric Current)
Credit given for Periodic Assessment/Portfolio based on joyful and experiential opportunities
